Examples.Title: Post actions
Examples.Description:
Perform a Data Manager action with the selected tasks and filters. Note: More complex actions require additional parameters in the request body. Call `GET api/actions?project=` to explore them.
Example: `GET api/actions?id=delete_tasks&project=1`

Examples.Title: Get actions
Examples.Description: Retrieve all the registered actions with descriptions that data manager can use.
Examples.Code:
### Get actions
# @name api_dm_actions_retrieve
GET {{host}}/api/dm/actions/?project={{project}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Actions retrieved successfully
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Create annotation
Examples.Description:
Add annotations to a task like an annotator does. The content of the result field depends on your
        labeling configuration. For example, send the following data as part of your POST
        request to send an empty annotation with the ID of the user who completed the task:

        ```json
        {
        "result": {},
        "was_cancelled": true,
        "ground_truth": true,
        "lead_time": 0,
        "task": 0
        "completed_by": 123
        }
        ```

Examples.Title: Get annotation by its ID
Examples.Description: Retrieve a specific annotation for a task using the annotation result ID.
Examples.Code:
### Get annotation by its ID
# @name api_annotations_retrieve
GET {{host}}/api/annotations/{{id}}/
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Retrieved annotation
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Get all task annotations
Examples.Description: List all annotations for a task.
Examples.Code:
### Get all task annotations
# @name api_tasks_annotations_list
GET {{host}}/api/tasks/{{id}}/annotations/?ordering={{ordering}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Annotation
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Update annotation
Examples.Description: Update existing attributes on an annotation.

Examples.Title: ✨ Get billing info
Examples.Description:
This endpoint is not available in Label Studio Community Edition. [Learn more about Label Studio Enterprise](https://humansignal.com/goenterprise)

Retrieve billing checks and feature flags for the active organization.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using LabelStudio;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: ✨ Get task agreement matrix
Examples.Description:
This endpoint is not available in Label Studio Community Edition. [Learn more about Label Studio Enterprise](https://humansignal.com/goenterprise)

Returns a pairwise agreement matrix between selected participants for a single task, averaged across all active dimensions or a single specified dimension.
Examples.Code:
### ✨ Get task agreement matrix
# @name api_projects_tasks_agreement_matrix_retrieve
GET {{host}}/api/projects/{{project_pk}}/tasks/{{task_pk}}/agreement-matrix?dimension={{dimension}}&selection={{selection}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Test prediction
Examples.Description: After you add an ML backend, call this API with the ML backend ID to run a test prediction on specific task data
Examples.Code:
### Test prediction
# @name api_ml_predict_test_create
POST {{host}}/api/ml/{{id}}/predict/test
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "auth_method": "NONE",
  "auto_update": true,
  "basic_auth_pass": "string",
  "basic_auth_user": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "error_message": "string",
  "extra_params": "string",
  "is_interactive": true,
  "model_version": "string",
  "project": 0,
  "state": "CO",
  "timeout": 0,
  "title": "string",
  "url": "string"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Predicting has successfully started.
# 500
# Description: Predicting error
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Train
Examples.Description:
After you add an ML backend, call this API with the ML backend ID to start training with 
        already-labeled tasks. 
        
        Get the ML backend ID by [listing the ML backends for a project](https://labelstud.io/api/#operation/api_ml_list).
Examples.Code:
### Train
# @name api_ml_train_create
POST {{host}}/api/ml/{{id}}/train
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "use_ground_truth": true
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Training has successfully started.
# 500
# Description: Training error
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: ✨ Bulk delete organization member tags
Examples.Description:
This endpoint is not available in Label Studio Community Edition. [Learn more about Label Studio Enterprise](https://humansignal.com/goenterprise)

Delete multiple member tags from the organization in bulk. Pass tag IDs via the `ids` query parameter (comma-separated or repeated). For backward compatibility, a JSON body with `ids` is still accepted.
Examples.Code:
using var client = new LabelStudioClient(apiKey);
var response = await client.AnnotationHistory.DeleteAsync();
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using LabelStudio;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Description: Overwrite prediction data by prediction ID.

Examples.Title: List predictions

# Description: Predictions list
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Update prediction
Examples.Description: Update prediction data by prediction ID.

Examples.Title: ✨ Create annotator weights for statistics
Examples.Description:
This endpoint is not available in Label Studio Community Edition. [Learn more about Label Studio Enterprise](https://humansignal.com/goenterprise)

Create annotator weights to be used in the annotation statistics for a project, such as when calculating kappa metrics for inter-annotator agreement. Affects dashboard-members stats, but old and unused.
Examples.Code:
### ✨ Create annotator weights for statistics
# @name api_projects_project_extra_params_create
POST {{host}}/api/projects/{{id}}/project-extra-params/
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "annotator_params": {
    "123": 1.0,
    "456": 0.75
  },
  "use_kappa": true
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Annotator weights created/updated
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: ✨ Bulk unassign project members
Examples.Description:
This endpoint is not available in Label Studio Community Edition. [Learn more about Label Studio Enterprise](https://humansignal.com/goenterprise)

Unassign project members in bulk. Pass selector fields via query parameters (`all`, `included`, `excluded`) and optional member filters (`search`, `role`, `tags`, `last_activity__gte`, `last_activity__lte`). For backward compatibility, a JSON body with bulk fields is still accepted.
Examples.Code:
using var client = new LabelStudioClient(apiKey);
var response = await client.AnnotationHistory.DeleteAsync();
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using LabelStudio;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: ✨ Get Inter-Annotator Agreement matrix
Examples.Description:
This endpoint is not available in Label Studio Community Edition. [Learn more about Label Studio Enterprise](https://humansignal.com/goenterprise)

Get Inter-Annotator Agreement (IAA) matrix for a project, showing agreement between all annotators.
Examples.Code:
### ✨ Get Inter-Annotator Agreement matrix
# @name api_projects_stats_IAA_retrieve
GET {{host}}/api/projects/{{id}}/stats/IAA?expand={{expand}}&per_label=false&std=false&task={{task}}
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Inter-Annotator Agreement matrix
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: ✨ Get label distribution counts
Examples.Description:
This endpoint is not available in Label Studio Community Edition. [Learn more about Label Studio Enterprise](https://humansignal.com/goenterprise)

Returns counts and percentages for requested label choices, from both annotations and predictions. Supports either pagination (`limit`, `offset`) or targeted fetches via explicit `choice_keys`.
Examples.Code:
### ✨ Get label distribution counts
# @name api_projects_stats_label_distribution_counts_retrieve
GET {{host}}/api/projects/{{id}}/stats/label-distribution/counts?choice_keys={{choice_keys}}&limit={{limit}}&offset=0
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Label distribution counts
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: ✨ Get label distribution structure
Examples.Description:
This endpoint is not available in Label Studio Community Edition. [Learn more about Label Studio Enterprise](https://humansignal.com/goenterprise)

Returns dimensions and flattened `choice_keys` for a project. Use this response to drive paginated or targeted calls to the label distribution counts endpoint.
Examples.Code:
### ✨ Get label distribution structure
# @name api_projects_stats_label_distribution_structure_retrieve
GET {{host}}/api/projects/{{id}}/stats/label-distribution/structure
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Label distribution structure
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: ✨ Bulk unassign workspace members
Examples.Description:
This endpoint is not available in Label Studio Community Edition. [Learn more about Label Studio Enterprise](https://humansignal.com/goenterprise)

Unassign workspace members in bulk. Pass selector fields via query parameters (`all`, `included`, `excluded`) and optional paginated-list filters (`search`, `ids`). For backward compatibility, a JSON body with bulk fields is still accepted.
Examples.Code:
using var client = new LabelStudioClient(apiKey);
var response = await client.AnnotationHistory.DeleteAsync();
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using LabelStudio;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.
